description: High-frequency PTE exam words curated from 20 years of PTE Academic exam analysis. These words appear repeatedly across Reading FIB, Listening FIB, WFD, and Essay tasks.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: present in large quantities; more than enough
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The region has abundant natural resources.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: correct in all details; free from errors
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists need accurate data to draw conclusions.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to change to suit different conditions or uses
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Animals must adapt to survive in changing environments.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Students need adequate time to prepare for exams.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to have an impact or influence on something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Climate change affects millions of people worldwide.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to examine in detail for purposes of explanation
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Researchers analyze data to find patterns.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: occurring once every year
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The company publishes an annual report.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a way of dealing with something; to come near
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: A new approach to teaching is needed.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to suppose something to be true without proof
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: We should not assume all students learn the same way.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: able to be used or obtained; at someone's disposal
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Free healthcare should be available to everyone.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: an advantage or profit gained from something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Exercise has many health benefits.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a task or situation that tests abilities; to dispute
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Climate change is one of the greatest challenges of our time.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: consisting of many different parts; difficult to understand
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The human brain is an extremely complex organ.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: worry or anxiety about something important
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Environmental pollution is a major global concern.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to give something (money, time, ideas) to a cause
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Technology contributes to economic growth.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to bring something into existence
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: New technologies create new job opportunities.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: extremely important; decisive
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Education is crucial for personal development.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to decrease in quantity or quality; to refuse
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Air quality has declined in many major cities.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to state the exact meaning of; to characterize
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: It is important to clearly define the problem before solving it.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to show evidence; to prove clearly
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Research demonstrates that exercise reduces stress.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to be controlled or determined by something else
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Economic development depends on political stability.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to cause something to occur in a particular way; to find out
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Genes determine many physical characteristics.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to grow or cause to grow and become more advanced
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Countries must develop sustainable energy sources.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to give out shares to several people; to spread over an area
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Food aid was distributed to affected communities.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the system of production, distribution, and trade in a country
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: A strong economy creates more employment opportunities.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: producing a desired or intended result
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: An effective leader motivates their team.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the natural world; surroundings or conditions
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: We must protect the environment for future generations.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to set up on a firm or permanent basis
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The government established new environmental laws.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: facts or information indicating whether something is true
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: There is strong evidence that smoking causes cancer.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to become or make larger or more extensive
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Companies seek to expand into new markets.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a circumstance or element that contributes to a result
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Diet is an important factor in maintaining good health.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to concentrate on something; center of interest
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Schools should focus on critical thinking skills.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to produce or create
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Solar panels generate clean electricity.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: relating to the whole world
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Global warming is an urgent issue.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to recognize or establish who or what something is
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists identified a new species in the Amazon.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a strong effect or influence on something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Technology has a huge impact on our daily lives.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to put a plan, decision, or agreement into effect
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The government implemented new policies to reduce poverty.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to make or become better
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Regular exercise improves both physical and mental health.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to become or make greater in amount or size
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Global temperatures have increased significantly.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to point out; to be a sign or symptom of
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Research indicates that stress affects health.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a single person; relating to one person
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Every individual has a responsibility to protect the planet.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the capacity to have an effect on others
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Media has a powerful influence on public opinion.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: an important topic or problem for debate or discussion
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Poverty is a major global issue.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to keep in an existing state; to preserve from failure
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: It is important to maintain a healthy lifestyle.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: important, serious, or significant
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Pollution is a major threat to biodiversity.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to be in charge of; to handle or control
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Good leaders manage their teams effectively.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a particular procedure for accomplishing something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: New teaching methods are being introduced in schools.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: relating to the present or recent times
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Modern technology has transformed communication.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: existing in or caused by nature; normal
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Natural disasters are increasing due to climate change.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to acquire or secure something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Students must obtain a degree to enter many professions.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to happen; to exist or be found
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Climate-related disasters occur more frequently now.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a favorable time or set of circumstances for action
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Education provides opportunities for social mobility.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a course of action adopted by government or institution
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Environmental policies must be strengthened globally.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: all the inhabitants of a particular area
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The global population is expected to reach 10 billion by 2050.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: having or showing the capacity to become something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Young people have the potential to change the world.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to stop something from happening
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Vaccination helps prevent the spread of disease.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: most important; main
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The primary cause of obesity is an unhealthy diet.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a series of actions or steps to achieve an end
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The manufacturing process has become more automated.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to make or create something; to cause to happen
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Factories produce millions of goods every day.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to actively encourage; to raise to a higher position
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Schools should promote creativity and critical thinking.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to make available; to supply
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Governments should provide free education to all citizens.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to make or become smaller in size or amount
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: We must reduce carbon emissions to combat climate change.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to think deeply; to show an image; to be a sign of
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Education systems reflect societal values.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to need something; to make something necessary
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Solving climate change requires international cooperation.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a supply of something useful; materials available
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Water is one of the world's most precious resources.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a consequence or outcome of something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Deforestation results in loss of biodiversity.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: important or large enough to be noticed
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: There has been a significant increase in global temperatures.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: a means of solving a problem or difficulty
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Renewable energy is a sustainable solution to pollution.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the way something is built or organized
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: The structure of DNA was discovered in 1953.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to put forward an idea for consideration; to indicate
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Evidence suggests that diet affects brain function.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to give assistance to; to bear the weight of
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Governments should support renewable energy projects.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: able to be maintained at a certain level without depleting resources
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Sustainable farming protects the environment.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Advances in technology are changing the workforce.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: following long-established customs or methods
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Traditional farming methods are being replaced by modern techniques.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: to comprehend the meaning or importance of something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: It is important to understand the causes of poverty.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: relating to a city or town
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Urban areas are growing as people move from villages.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: more than one; several different
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists study various factors that affect climate change.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: absolutely necessary or important
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Clean water is vital for human survival.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: found or distributed over a large area
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Smartphone use is now widespread globally.

basic_vocabulary.meaning: the right or opportunity to reach something
basic_vocabulary.example_sentence: Everyone deserves access to quality healthcare.

study_tips: Learn each word with its collocations — PTE Reading FIB tests collocations heavily
Practice words in context — write 2-3 sentences using each word
Group words by category to understand semantic fields
Focus on high-frequency words marked 'very high' first
Learn synonyms and antonyms — PTE Essay benefits from varied vocabulary
WFD (Write From Dictation) — focus on common academic words
Reading FIB — understand word forms: noun, verb, adjective, adverb
next_level: Advanced Vocabulary — coming next with 200+ words covering academic, scientific, and specialized PTE topics
